definition: Rising median voter age shifts the decisive electoral cohort to net transfer recipients, creating a self-reinforcing cycle: fiscal expansion → monetary accommodation → further expansion. Operates in democracies with ~1/20 counterexample rate. Only Germany's Schuldenbremse partially deviates, and it is under sustained pressure.
evidence: Median voter age: Japan ~55, Italy ~52, Germany ~49, US ~47
Every OECD country crossing 45% dependency experienced qualitative shift toward monetary-fiscal coordination
Germany's Schuldenbremse is the sole partial counterexample under sustained political pressure
France 2023 pension reform required bypassing parliament (Article 49.3), triggering largest protests in decades
open_questions: Does the mechanism operate at different speeds in different institutional contexts?
Can constitutional fiscal rules survive sustained demographic pressure?
What is the tipping point median voter age for the mechanism to activate?
